servinfo.title: Front Brake Caliper Disassembly and Reassembly

ptxt.intxrefpoff: Front Brake Caliper Removal and Installation

s1.ptxt: Clean surfaces around caliper with brake cleaner before disassembly.
Remove brake piston by blowing air into the hole for front brake flexible hose joint bolt.

attention2.ptxt: If you place your fingers in front of the piston when you push it out with compressed air, you may hurt your fingers.
Do not place your fingers in front of the piston when removing it using compressed air.

attention4.ptxt: Using compressed air that is under high pressure can force the disc brake piston quickly out of the cylinder and damage the piston.
Use a moderate air pressure to push the piston out of the cylinder slowly. In addition, place a cloth (1) in front of the piston to prevent it from being damaged.

s1.ptxt: Remove cylinder boot (1) and bleeder plug (2).
Remove piston seal (1) from caliper cylinder of front brake caliper (3) using flat-bladed screwdriver (2) or the like.

attention4.ptxt: If the inside surface of the cylinder (bore wall) and outside surface of piston are damaged, brake fluid leaks may result.
Be extremely careful not to damage the inside surface of the cylinder and outside surface of piston when removing the piston seal.

topic.title: Slide pin
topic.ptxt: Remove slide pins (1) and pin boots (2) from front brake caliper carrier (3).

attention1.ptxt: Failure to take proper precautions when assembling the brake caliper can result in brake system failure, which could lead an accident.

item.ptxt: Clean each part before installation using the same brake fluid as that in the master cylinder reservoir.
Never use any other brake fluid or volatile solvent.
Before installing the piston seal and cylinder boot to the cylinder, apply rubber grease included in the piston seal set or brake fluid to them.
Fit a new piston seal, securely into the groove of the cylinder making sure that it is not twisted.

attention4.ptxt: If you do not bleed air from the brake system after replacement of a brake pipe or hose, brake system performance may be affected.
Be sure to bleed the brake system referring to Air Bleeding of Brake System after replacement of a brake pipe or hose.

s1.ptxt: Perform the following steps before installing brake piston to front brake caliper.

s2.ptxt: Check entire periphery of brake piston and inside surface of cylinder for rust, corrosion and any other damage. If check result is not OK, replace front brake caliper.
After applying rubber grease included in piston seal set of supply parts or specified brake fluid to new piston seal (1), fit the seal into groove in cylinder taking care not to twist the seal.

spec.title: Brake fluid
spec.ptxt: Refer to reservoir cap of brake master cylinder or owner’s manual.

s2.ptxt: Apply rubber grease included in piston seal set of supply parts or specified brake fluid to new cylinder boot (1). And then install it onto brake piston as shown.

s1.ptxt: Fit cylinder boot with piston into boot groove in caliper cylinder by hand as shown below.
Insert brake piston into cylinder by hand and fit boot in boot groove on brake piston.

attention3.ptxt: Check that boot is securely fitted in boot grooves in piston and cylinder.

s1.ptxt: To confirm that boot is fitted in its groove in cylinder properly, pull piston a little outward without moving it fully out.
Insert piston into cylinder by hand.
Install bleeder plug to front brake caliper and tighten it to specified torque.
Check the following points before installing slide pin to front brake caliper carrier.

s2.ptxt: Check slide pins for rust, corrosion and other damage. Replace slide pin if defective.
Apply grease to inside of new pin boot (1).
Install pin boots (1) to front brake caliper carrier (2) with their ends directed as shown below.

attention3.ptxt: Use rubber grease whose viscosity varies very little even at –40°C (–40°F).

s1.ptxt: Install slide pin with three grooves (1) into upper side and slide pin without groove (2) into lower side of front brake caliper carrier (3) as shown.
